general.prompt: What decided to crash Philadelphia's July 4th concert so hard that everyone had to flee the Ben Franklin Parkway, only to party again after midnight?
options: A sudden Taylor Swift flash mob
A surprise visit from Kylian Mbappé
general.summary: Severe storms forced the evacuation of Philadelphia's Fourth of July celebration, which resumed after midnight.

general.prompt: Who rolled up to the National Mall after a storm delay, ready to give a speech while everyone else tried to get back in?
general.summary: Trump arrived at the National Mall for a speech after bad weather delayed the July 4th rally.

general.prompt: Who had their Rhode Island home lit up like a Christmas tree while fireworks went off on the 4th, probably judging everyone's pyrotechnics from inside?
general.summary: Taylor Swift's Rhode Island house had lights on as fireworks happened on July 4th.

general.prompt: What weather trouble kept ruining July 4th plans across the country, forcing evacuations from the National Mall to Philly?
options: A heatwave that melted the hot dogs
general.summary: Severe weather dampened July 4th celebrations and caused evacuations nationwide.

general.prompt: Which French soccer star scored a penalty kick in the World Cup Round of 16, inching within one goal of the all-time record held by Lionel Messi?
options: Taylor Swift in a referee uniform
general.summary: Kylian Mbappé scored his 19th career World Cup goal on a penalty kick, one behind Lionel Messi's record.

tech.prompt: Nintendo just sent a message to its fans that’s so wholesome, it probably came with a side of Mario pasta! What did the big N say it’s pushing for?
options: Nintendo shares a message with its fans
tech.summary: Nintendo shared a message with fans, emphasizing its commitment to originality in game development.

tech.prompt: Google’s new ad is asking: what if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace? How many years after the Declaration of Independence is this set?
options: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
tech.summary: A new Google commercial imagines the Founding Fathers using AI to write the Declaration of Independence, 250 years after its signing.

tech.prompt: Dragon Quest 11 S is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 with more details than a slime’s battle plan! How many graphical options will players get?
options: Dragon Quest 11 S details Nintendo Switch 2 frame rate and resolution
tech.summary: Square Enix revealed Dragon Quest 11 S on Switch 2 will include two graphical options, with details on frame rate and resolution.

tech.prompt: Resident Evil fans, get ready to scream—but also mark your calendars! What is the name of the new release dropping on July 30?
options: New Official Resident Evil Release Confirmed for July 30
tech.summary: A new Resident Evil release titled 'Resident Evil Requiem' is confirmed for July 30.

tech.prompt: Got a scratched CD that skips like a broken record? Well, the nerds have spoken! What’s the hot new open-source trend for saving your discs?
tech.summary: Disk polishing has gone open source, offering a way to repair scratched optical media like CDs and DVDs.

business.prompt: What's the one thing that'll make your wallet weep faster than a California avocado shortage?
options: A speeding ticket for going 5 over
A parking violation in downtown LA
The staggering cost California drivers pay for getting a DUI
business.summary: California drivers face the second-steepest car insurance hike in the nation after a DUI conviction.

business.prompt: Why did the PECO workers walk off the job during a heat wave? Was it for a better tan?
options: PECO workers strike starts amid Fourth of July heat wave
business.summary: PECO workers went on strike over wages and benefits, but the company says it has plans to keep service running.

business.prompt: What delightful surprise awaited passengers on a luxury cruise to Alaska?
options: A free upgrade to the captain's suite
Stomach-sick passengers and strict disease protocols
Exclusive | Passenger on virus-stricken California cruise ship reveals stunning details of chaotic high-seas journey
business.summary: A passenger on a virus-stricken California cruise ship described sick passengers, strict protocols, and fear on board during a 20-day Alaskan voyage.

business.prompt: Where did a 78-year-old man meet his end in a fiery crash—and no, it's not a scene from a disaster movie?
options: FHP: 78-year-old man killed in fiery crash at McDonald’s parking lot in Tallahassee
business.summary: A 78-year-old man was killed in a fiery crash at a McDonald's parking lot in Tallahassee on Saturday.

business.prompt: What weird food request did a Denver bartender get from a couple that's not on the menu?
options: Denver bartender serves couple. Then the woman makes a strange food request: ‘There’s a couple ways you can get it’
business.summary: A Denver bartender served a couple, and the woman made a strange request about a baked potato.

sports.prompt: Who lit up the 4th of July like a human firework and sent the Astros home happy?
options: A random fan in the outfield
sports.summary: Yordan Alvarez hit a two-run walk-off home run in the bottom of the ninth inning to cap a wild Astros comeback.

sports.prompt: Which NFL receiver might need a GPS to find his way back to the Commanders' good graces?
sports.summary: Brandon Aiyuk may be burning his bridge to the Washington Commanders after going after rookie quarterback Jayden Daniels.

sports.prompt: What did England's soccer coach say about Mexico that made everyone put down their sombreros?
options: That the tacos are too spicy
That it was nicer than expected
sports.summary: Thomas Tuchel dismissed concerns about England's treatment in Mexico, saying it was nicer than expected and local fans are friendly and respectful.

sports.prompt: Which rookie baller was the star of the show in his summer league debut for the Jazz?
options: A saxophone player from the halftime show
sports.summary: Darryn Peterson led the Utah Jazz to a win over the Atlanta Hawks in Salt Lake City Summer League action.

sports.prompt: Which French soccer superstar is breathing down Lionel Messi's neck in the World Cup goal race?
sports.summary: Kylian Mbappé scored his 19th World Cup goal on a penalty kick, moving within one of record holder Lionel Messi.

science.prompt: Space is playing cosmic pranks on us! Two enormous structures — the Giant Arc and the Big Ring — were found hanging out in the same part of deep space, like universe-sized best friends. An astronomer says current cosmology can't even explain this pairing. What does that mean for our understanding of the universe?
options: The universe is just showing off its geometry skills
Two enormous structures — the Giant Arc and the Big Ring — appear in the same region of deep space at the same cosmic distance
Aliens built them as interstellar billboards
Our models of the universe need a major rethink
science.summary: Two massive structures, the Giant Arc and the Big Ring, were discovered in the same deep-space region at the same distance, puzzling astronomers who say current cosmology can't explain them.

science.prompt: Plot twist! Dinosaurs weren't the 'washed-up has-beens' scientists thought they were before the asteroid hit. A 2025 study found fossils in New Mexico showing they were actually living it up in thriving ecosystems. So what was their vibe right before the big bang?
options: For much of the past century, scientists believed dinosaurs were already in decline when the asteroid struck
They were flourishing like a Jurassic block party
They were busy building tiny bunkers
They were all on a strict pre-asteroid diet
science.summary: A 2025 study in Science found that dinosaurs were flourishing in diverse ecosystems just before the asteroid strike 66 million years ago, contradicting the long-held belief they were in decline.

science.prompt: Data centers on Earth guzzle up to 30% of their energy just to stay cool, plus millions of liters of water! But in space, there's no air or rivers to dump heat into. How do orbital data centers keep their chill?
options: They use giant radiator panels that bleed heat into space as infrared light
They just open a window and let the vacuum cool things down
Cooling a data centre on Earth can consume 10 to 30 per cent of its energy budget
They rely on mini ice factories powered by solar panels
science.summary: Cooling a data center on Earth uses significant energy and water, but in orbit, heat is managed using vast radiator panels that release infrared light into space.

science.prompt: Talk about a long-distance call! Voyager 1, launched in 1977, is so far that a radio signal takes over 22 hours to reach it. And guess what? It's still sending data back, running on a power source no bigger than a car battery. What keeps this ancient probe going?
options: A nuclear power source the size of a car battery
A hamster wheel powered by space hamsters
Solar panels that somehow work billions of miles from the Sun
The Voyager 1 probe, launched in 1977, is now so far from Earth that a radio signal takes over 22 hours to reach it
science.summary: Voyager 1, launched in 1977, is over 22 light-hours away and still operating on a nuclear power source the size of a car battery, sending data from interstellar space.

science.prompt: Anxiety might be more than just a mood—it could be in your genes! A giant study of nearly 700,000 people found 74 spots in the genome linked to anxiety, with 39 never seen before. What's the big takeaway for how we treat anxiety?
options: A genetic analysis of nearly 700,000 people identified 74 distinct locations in the human genome tied to anxiety
Anxiety has a strong biological foundation, not just a mood problem
Everyone with anxiety should start worrying about their gene pool
Worrying can now be blamed entirely on your parents' DNA
science.summary: A genetic analysis of nearly 700,000 people identified 74 genome locations linked to anxiety, 39 of which were new, suggesting a stronger biological basis for the condition.
